Interior Plaster in Williamson County, TX

Star Stucco of Austin brings professional interior plaster services to Williamson County, serving homeowners in Round Rock, Cedar Park, Georgetown, and the surrounding communities. From polished Venetian accents to full-home skim coats, we deliver wall finishes that elevate every room.

Get a Free EstimateCall (512) 706-9699

Custom Plaster Finishes for Williamson County Interiors

Williamson County has seen rapid residential growth over the past decade, and many of the newer subdivisions in Round Rock, Leander, and Liberty Hill feature production-grade drywall with basic textures. Homeowners looking to set their interiors apart are turning to plaster finishes that add warmth, depth, and lasting quality to living spaces that otherwise feel identical to the house next door.

Georgetown's historic downtown square and the older neighborhoods surrounding it contain homes with original plaster walls that need careful restoration. At the same time, the custom homes being built west of Cedar Park in the Hill Country foothills often call for high-end decorative finishes that complement natural stone and hardwood throughout the home.

Our crews work regularly across Williamson County and understand the fast pace of construction here. We coordinate closely with builders, designers, and homeowners to keep interior plaster work on schedule, whether we are finishing a single powder room or an entire new build.

What's Included With Our Interior Plaster in Williamson County

Every project includes expert craftsmanship, quality materials, and a commitment to lasting results.

Design Consultation

We visit your Williamson County home to assess existing wall conditions, discuss finish preferences, and walk through sample panels that show the full range of plaster options available.

Surface Preparation

All surfaces are inspected, repaired if needed, and coated with appropriate primer. We mask off trim, cabinetry, and flooring so the workspace stays clean throughout the project.

Base Coat Application

A base coat formulated for interior use is spread uniformly across each wall, establishing the bond layer that supports the decorative finish above it.

Decorative Finish Application

Finish plaster is applied in controlled passes, with each layer troweled and burnished to develop the final texture and sheen specified during your consultation.

Color Matching & Tinting

We mix pigments into the plaster body so the color is integral, not painted on. This ensures long-term consistency and makes touch-ups virtually invisible years later.

Protective Sealing & Cleanup

A clear or tinted sealer is applied to guard the finished surface against moisture and everyday wear. All masking is removed, and the area is left clean and ready to enjoy.

Common Interior Plaster Needs in Williamson County

Homeowners across Williamson County request interior plaster for a variety of reasons. Here are the projects we handle most often in this area.

Smooth Wall Finishes

Builder-grade knockdown textures are the default in most Williamson County subdivisions. A smooth skim coat eliminates that texture and gives your walls a sleek, contemporary surface that photographs and paints beautifully.

Decorative Plaster Accents

Accent walls in family rooms, behind media centers, and around fireplaces are popular upgrade projects in Round Rock and Cedar Park homes. Decorative plaster adds visual interest without the maintenance of wallpaper.

Venetian Plaster

Georgetown's stately homes and the custom builds appearing along the Ronald Reagan corridor often incorporate Venetian plaster in formal dining rooms and foyers where the polished, layered finish draws the eye.

Interior Wall Restoration

The older homes around Georgetown's courthouse square and Sun City sometimes have damaged plaster or deteriorating wall surfaces that benefit from careful restoration rather than demolition.

Custom Textures for Feature Walls

Whether it is a subtle metallic shimmer in a home office or a rough-troweled Old World look in a wine room, we create custom textures that turn a single wall into the centerpiece of the space.

Skim Coating Over Textured Walls

Williamson County homeowners preparing homes for sale or simply updating their interior style frequently request a smooth skim coat over existing heavy textures to modernize the look.

Why Choose Us for Interior Plaster in Williamson County?

  • Skilled plasterers who deliver artisan-quality finishes in both production homes and custom residences
  • Full range of interior plaster styles, from understated smooth coats to dramatic layered Venetian finishes
  • Accurate color formulation that stays true over time and coordinates with your existing design scheme
  • Meticulous interior protection — every surface in your home is shielded before work begins
  • Detail-oriented finishing that produces clean edges, smooth transitions, and uniform coverage
  • Project plans adapted to your home's architecture, whether it is a new build in Leander or a restoration in Georgetown

Licensed & Insured

Full liability coverage and workers' compensation for every project in Williamson County.

20+ Years Experience

Decades of hands-on stucco work across Central Texas residential and commercial properties.

Certified Applicators

Certified by Sto Corp, Senergy/BASF, Master Wall, Dryvit, and Parex LaHabra for the highest quality results.

Our Interior Plaster Process

A straightforward approach from start to finish — no surprises, no shortcuts.

1

Design Consultation

We meet at your Williamson County property to review the walls, discuss your vision, and present finish samples that let you compare textures and colors side by side.

2

Surface Preparation & Priming

Walls are repaired, sanded where necessary, and treated with a bonding primer. Furnishings and floors are draped so the space is ready for plaster application.

3

Plaster Application & Finishing

Our team applies each coat methodically, building depth and consistency across the wall surface. Venetian and polished finishes receive additional burnishing passes for full reflective depth.

4

Final Review & Touch-Ups

We inspect every wall under natural and artificial light with you present. Any imperfections are corrected, sealer is applied, and the room is returned to its normal state.
